Root.title: 1-Day Beijing Highlights Itinerary (Classic First-Timer Route)
Root.starting_area: Central Beijing (hotel area; easy access by subway)
Root.pace: Moderate (full day with transfers; start early to avoid crowds)

assumptions.assumption: First-time visitor focusing on classic highlights
Using subway plus walking, with taxis/ride-hail as needed
Forbidden City tickets booked in advance; note it is closed Mondays
Weather may affect outdoor stops; backup indoor museum option included

Day.theme: Imperial core + viewpoints + hutongs + Temple of Heaven

Meal.suggested_spot: Local breakfast stall or small shop near your hotel

Notes.tip: Start early to beat crowds at major sights.
Notes.backup_option: If you prefer seated breakfast, pick a nearby cafe-style chain in Dongcheng/Chaoyang.
Notes.time_saver: Eat close to your hotel to avoid morning detours.

Stop.name: Forbidden City (Palace Museum)
Stop.what_to_do: Focus on the main halls and the central north-south axis for a first visit.
Stop.tickets_or_entry: Timed ticket required; book in advance (closed Mondays). Bring passport/ID.

Transport_Leg.route_hint: Subway to Tiananmen East or Tiananmen West, then a short walk toward the museum entrance area.

Notes.tip: Carry passport/ID for entry checks; arrive early for smoother entry.
Notes.backup_option: If tickets are unavailable or weather is poor, consider an indoor museum such as the National Museum of China or the Capital Museum.
Notes.time_saver: Prioritize the central axis highlights rather than trying to cover every courtyard.

Stop.name: Jingshan Park
Stop.what_to_do: Climb to the pavilion for one of the best panoramas over the Forbidden City.
Stop.tickets_or_entry: Park ticket may be required (small fee).
Stop.address_or_area: Jingshan Park (north of the Forbidden City)

Transport_Leg.route_hint: Head north from the Forbidden City area; walking is feasible depending on exit and crowds.

Notes.tip: Bring water; the climb is short but can feel steep in summer heat.
Notes.backup_option: If you are tired, shorten the park visit and spend more time in hutongs later.
Notes.time_saver: Go directly to the main viewpoint pavilion for the iconic photo.

Stop.name: Lunch in Shichahai/Houhai area
Stop.what_to_do: Refuel with a classic Beijing dish before exploring nearby hutongs.

Transport_Leg.estimated_time: 15-25 min (taxi) or longer by subway + walk
Transport_Leg.route_hint: Taxi/ride-hail is often simplest; subway + walking works if traffic is heavy.
Transport_Leg.cost_hint: Low to medium (depending on mode and traffic)

Meal.what_to_order: Peking duck or Beijing zhajiangmian (noodles with soybean paste)

Notes.tip: If you want Peking duck, consider reserving or choosing a well-reviewed spot nearby.
Notes.backup_option: If it is crowded, pick a smaller noodle/dumpling shop in the hutongs.
Notes.time_saver: Eat close to where you will start the hutong walk to avoid extra transfers.

Stop.name: Hutongs (Shichahai/Houhai)
Stop.what_to_do: Stroll lanes and courtyards; enjoy lakeside views around Shichahai/Houhai.
Stop.tickets_or_entry: N/A (public lanes); any paid courtyard sites optional.

Notes.tip: Keep an eye out for quiet side lanes away from the busiest lakeside strip.
Notes.backup_option: If weather turns bad, head to a nearby tea house or an indoor museum later in the day.
Notes.time_saver: Stick to a simple loop walk starting near your lunch spot.

Stop.name: Temple of Heaven
Stop.what_to_do: See the Hall of Prayer for Good Harvests and enjoy the park atmosphere near sunset.
Stop.tickets_or_entry: Park entry; additional ticket may be required for main halls (varies by season/ticket type).
Stop.address_or_area: Tiantan Park / Temple of Heaven area

Transport_Leg.route_hint: Subway option: aim for Line 8 toward Tiantan East Gate area; taxi is direct but traffic-dependent.

Notes.tip: Late afternoon light is great for photos; allow time to walk between gates and the main hall.
Notes.backup_option: If it is raining, shorten the park portion and shift time to dinner or an indoor museum.
Notes.time_saver: Enter near the area that best matches the Hall of Prayer route to reduce walking.

Stop.name: Dinner in Wangfujing/Dongcheng (or nearby)
Stop.what_to_do: Choose an easy, flexible dinner area with many options after a full day.
Stop.address_or_area: Wangfujing / Dongcheng (or convenient to your hotel)

Transport_Leg.route_hint: Use subway for predictability at peak traffic; taxi is convenient if you are tired.

Meal.suggested_spot: Restaurants in Wangfujing/Dongcheng with many choices

Notes.tip: Pick a place with short waits or make a reservation if dining at peak time.
Notes.backup_option: If Wangfujing is too crowded, eat near your hotel or along a convenient subway line.
Notes.time_saver: Choose a restaurant close to the subway exit to minimize walking when tired.
